ogTitle: Condamine vs Condamine Farms: Climate Safety

heading: Condamine vs Condamine Farms — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Condamine, Queensland and Condamine Farms, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Condamine has a lower overall climate risk score (25/100) compared to Condamine Farms (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Condamine or Condamine Farms safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Condamine has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Condamine Farms scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Condamine scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Condamine Farms scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Condamine or Condamine Farms?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Condamine has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
